data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department has initiated the drafting of the GENIUS Act and is soliciting opinions on stablecoin regulation.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department recently launched the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANPRM) process to formally implement the GENIUS Act (U.S. Stablecoin National Innovation Guidance and Establishment Act), signed by President Trump. This marks the beginning of the substantive regulatory phase for stablecoin legislation. The Treasury Department is soliciting comments on details such as issuer restrictions, sanctions compliance, anti-money laundering requirements, the balance between federal and state regulation, tax issues, and custody of reserve assets. The public and crypto companies can submit feedback by October 20th. The GENIUS Act is the first crypto legislation to become law, and the Treasury Department's move aims to further refine the regulatory framework and complement the currently under discussion Digital Asset Market Clarity Act. (CoinDesk)

data.title: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a temporary spending bill, and the Senate may trigger a fierce confrontation
data.description: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a temporary spending bill aimed at averting a federal government shutdown on October 1st, but this also sets up a fierce clash in the Senate later on Friday. Democrats are expected to reject the bill on the grounds that "healthcare spending needs to be increased." House Speaker Johnson overcame the dissatisfaction of some Republican colleagues and pushed the bill through with a vote of 217 in favor and 212 against. House Democrats voted almost unanimously against the bill. The Senate plans to simultaneously consider the bill submitted by the House and the "alternative version of the temporary spending bill" proposed by Democratic leader Schumer (which plans to provide funding for the government until October 31st) later on Friday. Both versions of the bill are expected to be rejected, which means that the U.S. government will be stuck in a shutdown crisis less than two weeks before the October 1st deadline. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Treasury Seeks Public Input on GENIUS Act Preliminary Rules
data.description: According to Foresight News, the U.S. Treasury Department has initiated a public comment period for the preliminary rule-making steps of the GENIUS Act. This period aims to gather information that will assist in developing more detailed proposals in the future. The deadline for submitting comments is October 20.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: The US stablecoin market may have fallen into a zero-sum game
data.description: Following the passage of US stablecoin legislation, market competition has intensified. Tether plans to launch the fully compliant USAT stablecoin, while fintech giants such as PayPal and Robinhood have also entered the market. In response, Circle is developing its own proprietary blockchain, Arc, to enhance the competitiveness of USDC. A recent JPMorgan Chase research report indicates that the $270 billion stablecoin market may face a zero-sum game. The report shows that despite significant growth in the stablecoin market, its share of the total cryptocurrency market capitalization has remained below 8% since 2020. Analysts believe that unless the overall cryptocurrency market expands, new entrants may only redistribute existing market share rather than bring about substantial growth. (CoinDesk)

data.title: ARK Invest Acquires Significant Stake in Solmate
data.description: According to Foresight News, ARK Invest has made a substantial investment in Solmate by purchasing shares worth just under $162 million in the open market. This follows their participation in a $300 million funding round for the company. The investment highlights ARK Invest's continued interest in Solmate's growth and potential.

data.title: International Commercial Settlements' Crypto Joint Venture to Purchase $10.9 Million Worth of Crypto Mining Hardware
data.description: According to an announcement from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, International Commercial Settlement (00147.HK) announced that the buyer, Keen Golden Limited (a 70%-owned joint venture established by the company to conduct cryptocurrency-related businesses), has entered into sales and purchase agreements with the sellers, Bitmain Technologies and Tecnoenergia Capital, for the sale and purchase of cryptocurrency mining hardware and other equipment or commodities, with a total consideration (after tax) of approximately US$10.9 million.
